name: grep_tool
summary: Search file contents by substring under the working directory.
Returns file paths, matching lines, or per-file counts depending on `output_mode`. Skips `.git`, `target`, `node_modules`, `vendor` automatically.
constraints: ALWAYS use this tool for content search. NEVER invoke `grep` or `rg` via bash_tool.
Use the `glob` parameter to scope by file type (`*.rs`, `*.ts`); cheaper than searching the whole tree.

Pair with `read_file_tool` once you have a hit to inspect surrounding code. In content mode, long lines are truncated to ~200 bytes around the match; use `read_file_tool` with `col_offset`/`col_limit` to read the full context.
anti_patterns: To find files by *name*, not contents ‚Äî use `glob_tool`.
For open-ended searches that need multiple rounds ‚Äî delegate to `agent_tool`.
cautions: IMPORTANT: matches are substring, not regex. Pass the literal text you expect to find.
IMPORTANT: results are capped (`max_results`, default 100); if you suspect more, raise the cap or narrow `pattern` rather than re-running.
output: Plain text. Format depends on `output_mode`: `files` (default) ‚Äî one path per line; `content` ‚Äî match lines use `<path>:<line_no>:<col>: <line>` (1-based column of first match), context-only lines use `<path>:<line_no>: <line>`; `count` ‚Äî `<path>: <count>` per file.

pattern.description: Substring to search for. Literal match; not a regex.

path.description: Directory or file to search under (default: `.`).

glob.description: File-name filter (e.g. `*.rs`, `*.{ts,tsx}`). Applied before content search.

output_mode.description: What to return: `files` (default) ‚Äî distinct paths that contain the match; `content` ‚Äî matching lines with file path, line number, and surrounding context; `count` ‚Äî match count per file.

context_lines.description: Lines of context before and after each match (default: 0). Only used when `output_mode` is `content`.

case_insensitive.description: Match without regard to case (default: false).

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 100). Applies to whichever unit `output_mode` produces.
